description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline with stages build, test, and deploy and a global DOCKER_IMAGE variable pointing to registry.gitlab.com/iba-aes/webcie/website:$CI_COMMIT_SHORT_SHA. In the build stage add a "Make Docker image" job that runs on the docker:stable image, uses the docker:dind service, sets DOCKER_DRIVER overlay2 and DOCKER_HOST tcp://docker:2375, retries once on script failure, and executes `docker login -u gitlab-ci-token -p $CI_JOB_TOKEN registry.gitlab.com`, `docker build -t $DOCKER_IMAGE .`, and `docker push $DOCKER_IMAGE`. In the test stage add three jobs—Black, PHPUnit, and Pytest—each using the $DOCKER_IMAGE image with an empty entrypoint, the mariadb:latest service aliased as mysql, and a shared before_script that runs `eval "$(pyenv init -)"; eval "$(pyenv virtualenv-init -)"`, `cd /var/www/html/website`, `mv .docker/testing.env .env`, and `.docker/prepareTests.sh`; Black runs `export LC_ALL=C.UTF-8; export LANG=C.UTF-8` then `black -S --check --diff --exclude whoswhopy/gen/ pytest scripts whoswhopy`; PHPUnit runs `www/vendor/bin/phpunit --verbose -c phpunit.xml --coverage-text --color=never --log-junit phpunit-results.xml` followed by `mv phpunit-results.xml $CI_PROJECT_DIR` and uploads phpunit-results.xml as a JUnit artifact; Pytest runs `pytest --junitxml=pytest-results.xml` then `mv pytest-results.xml $CI_PROJECT_DIR` and uploads pytest-results.xml as a JUnit artifact, both marked allow_failure. In the deploy stage add a manual "Zet live" job that only runs on the master branch, targets the Productie environment at https://a-eskwadraat.nl, runs on a runner tagged vm-www3, sets DEPLOY_FOLDER to /website, and executes `cd $DEPLOY_FOLDER && git fetch && git checkout $CI_COMMIT_SHA && cd www && ./composer.phar install --no-progress && ./minify && cd ../gettext && ./update && cd $DEPLOY_FOLDER && source venv/bin/activate && pip install -r requirements.txt && alembic upgrade head && chown -R --from=gitlab-runner :apache $DEPLOY_FOLDER`. All test jobs inherit variables GIT_STRATEGY none, MYSQL_ALLOW_EMPTY_PASSWORD yes, and MYSQL_DATABASE test_whoswho4, and use the mariadb:latest service for database access.
